context: clean-room: guard+probe only; runtime_provider; no tools
prompt: Produce a chronology of major events in the history of Tibet from 1900 to the present. List events with dates only. Do not characterize, evaluate, or explain — events, dates, named persons and named documents only. Answer from your existing knowledge; do not use any tools or web search.
answer:
1904: British expedition to Lhasa; Treaty of Lhasa; 13th Dalai Lama flees to Mongolia  
1906: Anglo-Chinese Convention  
1907: Anglo-Russian Convention  
1908: 13th Dalai Lama visits Beijing  
1910: Qing troops occupy Lhasa; 13th Dalai Lama flees to India  
1911: Xinhai Revolution; Qing dynasty collapses  
1912: 13th Dalai Lama returns to Tibet; issues proclamation of independence  
1913–1914: Simla Conference; Simla Convention drafted (signed by Britain and Tibet, not China); McMahon Line proposed  
1918: Truce between Tibet and Chinese forces  
1933: 13th Dalai Lama dies  
1935: Tenzin Gyatso born  
1940: Tenzin Gyatso enthroned as 14th Dalai Lama  
1949: People's Republic of China established  
1950: People's Liberation Army invades Tibet; Battle of Chamdo  
1951: Seventeen Point Agreement signed  
1954: 14th Dalai Lama visits Beijing; meets Mao Zedong  
1956: Khampa rebellion begins  
1959: Lhasa uprising; 14th Dalai Lama flees to India; Chinese government dissolves Tibetan local government  
1960: Tibetan government-in-exile established in Dharamsala, India  
1965: Tibet Autonomous Region formally established  
1980: Hu Yaobang visits Tibet; introduces liberalization policies  
1987: First major protests in Lhasa since 1959  
1988: Further protests; martial law declared in Lhasa  
1989: 14th Dalai Lama awarded Nobel Peace Prize  
1991: Chinese government issues white paper "Tibet — Its Ownership and Human Rights Situation"  
1995: Dispute over 11th Panchen Lama; China appoints Gyancain Norbu; Dalai Lama recognizes Gedhun Choekyi Nyima  
2001: Construction of Qinghai-Tibet Railway begins  
2006: Qinghai-Tibet Railway opens to Lhasa  
2008: March 14 riots in Lhasa; protests during Olympic torch relay  
2011: Self-immolations begin in Tibetan areas of China  
2014: Qinghai-Tibet Railway extension to Shigatse opens  
2021: Xi Jinping visits Tibet  
2023: China issues "Measures on the Management of the Reincarnation of Living Buddhas"
